description_long: This object is a large fixed-blade knife with a straight, single-edged blade made of metallic material, likely steel, featuring a sharp point and a flat spine. The blade is attached to a robust, cylindrical handle that appears to be wrapped or coated in a dark brown material, possibly wood or synthetic, with a visible metallic pommel at the end. A simple, flat crossguard separates the blade from the handle, providing hand protection. The knife is accompanied by a matching sheath, also brown, designed to fully enclose the blade for safe storage and carrying. The sheath has a rectangular profile and appears to be made of a rigid or semi-rigid material, possibly leather or plastic. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, making it suitable for practical use in urban or outdoor environments.
description: A large, straight-bladed knife with a brown cylindrical handle, flat crossguard, and matching brown sheath. The design is functional and unadorned, emphasizing utility.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the knife and sheath, highlighting the straight blade, brown handle, and flat crossguard.
description_view_1: End-on view showing the circular cross-section of the handle and sheath, with the blade edge barely visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again displaying the straight blade and sheath, with the handle and guard clearly visible.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the flatness of the blade and sheath, and the alignment of the handle and guard.
